mnemonics.title: View Creation Steps
mnemonics.content: To remember view syntax: CREATE VIEW followed by name, AS keyword, then SELECT query - think 'CVNAS' = Create View Name AS Select

mnemonics.title: Updatable View Conditions
mnemonics.content: For updatable views: S - Single table only, N - No aggregates, G - No GROUP BY, H - No HAVING, D - No DISTINCT - think 'SNNGH' or 'SING' = Single table, no Groups/Distinct/Aggregates/Having

acronyms.usage: Standard API for accessing multiple database systems uniformly
SQL commands for defining database structures like CREATE, DROP
SQL commands for manipulating data like INSERT, UPDATE, DELETE
Java-based database connectivity standard (similar to ODBC for Java)
Set of functions allowing applications to interact with databases

visualTips.visualization: Draw a layered pyramid: Top (Application) -> Driver Manager -> ODBC Driver -> Bottom (Data Source/DBMS). Arrows flow downward for requests, upward for results.
Imagine a window (view) in a house (database). You see data through it but don't own what's inside - it's a perspective, not storage.
Regular view = live TV streaming (data fetched when you watch). Materialized view = recorded video (saved and played back).

analogies.analogy: Like a restaurant menu - it presents options (data) but doesn't contain the actual food (base table data). You see what's available without seeing the kitchen.
Like a universal phone charger that works across different phone brands - translates power (requests) into the right format for each device (database).
Like a contact saved in your phone - stores all connection details (name, number, address) so you don't have to remember them each time.
Like a projection screen - you can see through it and even modify what's behind it. Non-updatable views are like watching a recorded video - you can only view, not change.
